implementation_notes:
Made four targeted changes to agent_invoker.py:
1. Replaced the warning+return block in _kill_child_claude_processes() (line 815) with a call to self._kill_child_processes_fallback(my_pid).
2. Added _kill_child_processes_fallback() after _is_descendant_of() (line 915): tries psutil first via ImportError guard, falls back to pgrep.
3. Added _kill_child_processes_psutil() (line 930): uses psutil_mod passed as argument to avoid top-level import, walks parent.children(recursive=True), filters by name containing 'claude' or 'node', calls child.terminate().
4. Added _kill_child_processes_pgrep() (line 970): uses pgrep -P to enumerate direct children and one level of grandchildren, then ps -p to check names, sends SIGTERM via os.kill(). Handles FileNotFoundError (pgrep absent), TimeoutExpired, OSError, ProcessLookupError, and PermissionError.
All existing Linux /proc code (lines 819-879) is unchanged. The Any type was already imported at line 15. subprocess is re-imported locally inside _kill_child_processes_pgrep as specified.
requirements_addressed: _kill_child_claude_processes() terminates child Claude CLI processes on macOS
No new hard dependency added (psutil optional, with fallback)
The existing Linux /proc-based path is preserved as the primary approach

completion_promises.criterion_text: _kill_child_claude_processes() terminates child Claude CLI processes on macOS
completion_promises.evidence: Non-Linux branch now calls _kill_child_processes_fallback(my_pid) instead of logging a warning and returning. The fallback tries psutil first, then pgrep+ps, both of which are available on macOS. Both paths send SIGTERM to child processes whose names contain 'claude' or 'node'.

The existing /proc-based path (lines 819-879) is completely unchanged. The only modification to _kill_child_claude_processes() itself is replacing the 5-line warning+return block with a 2-line fallback call that only executes when /proc does not exist.

completion_promises.criterion_text: No new hard dependency added (psutil optional, with fallback)
completion_promises.evidence: psutil is imported inside _kill_child_processes_fallback() with a try/except ImportError that falls back to _kill_child_processes_pgrep(). It is never imported at the module level. pgrep and ps are standard macOS/BSD utilities requiring no installation.

completion_promises.criterion_text: The existing Linux /proc-based path is preserved as the primary approach
completion_promises.evidence: The /proc path remains the first branch executed in _kill_child_claude_processes(). It runs unconditionally on Linux where /proc exists. The new fallback methods are only reached when /proc is absent.
